# Page 1^page=1
This document contains technical data subject to EAR. U.S. ECCN: EAR99 OTIS Engineering Center Berlin Software Basic Data GCS - GECB Service Tool Reference List No.: GP330782JAH_REF SCN: GP330782JAH Page: 51 / 102 Date: 2023-11-16 8 Event Logging The table below lists all possible event logging messages of the Service Tool. The events are devided into the following classes: I Info W Warning F Fault E Elite - Visible only in the ALL Menu Event Trace L Log - Visible only in the GECB Event Log event class reason related config savedRuns number of runs since the events were last cleared - savedMins number of elapsed minutes since the events were last cleared - Operating System 0000 Task Timing W Dummy task has not been called within timeout. Software has been restarted. - 0001 S/W Reset W watchdog circuit on GECB was triggered. Software has been restarted. - 0002 Illegal Int W illegal interupt was executed - 0003 CcanTxFull W Car bus CAN messages cannot be sent - 0004 CcanBusOff F Car bus CAN communication failure. Check CAN termination. Check if CAN wiring in too close to devices that gener- ate disturbances. - 0006 StackCheck F Stack Violation was detected. Software has been restarted. - 0007 SCN Error F Wrong Software.  order a new software with correct partnumber. - 0008 TimerSync F Internal software timers have not been updated correctly. Software has been restarted. - 0009 Task Fail F Application software doesn’t run properly. Software has been restarted. - 0010 DrvCommErr F No CAN connection to the drive CAN Drive: DRV-TYP<100 0011 SpbCommErr W No CAN connection to the SPB System with SPBC: SPB-TYP=0,1 0013 Event Fail W Not implemented 0012 Power On I Board has been powered on. - 0014 PowerOffOn I Short Failure of PowerSupply. -

# Page 2^page=2
This document contains technical data subject to EAR. U.S. ECCN: EAR99 OTIS Engineering Center Berlin Software Basic Data GCS - GECB Service Tool Reference List No.: GP330782JAH_REF SCN: GP330782JAH Page: 52 / 102 Date: 2023-11-16 event class reason related config 0015 Spurious Int W A spurious interrupt occurred (can only happen with a bad software implementa- tion). - 0016 S/W Restart W Reset with unknown reason - 0017 Trace Error F bad value in the eventTraceControl buffer - 0018 Bad E2P W Different addresses found for “mirrored I/Os” (e.g. IO0032 and IO3000)  Check all level- and opening-related I/Os I/O 0019 MissedLvInt F lv1 and lv2 edge was missed PRS with 1LV+2LV: EN-CARIO=0, DZ-TYP=1,2 0020 RemoteReset I System was reset by Elite Service expert - 0021 Time Adjust I Time was changed using SVT function - 0022 Time Summer I Time was adjusted for summer time - 0023 Time Winter I Time was adjusted for winter time - 0024 Time NotSet W Time has not yet been set - 0025 ExternReset W The external watchdog caused a reset - 0026 Time NmsAdj E Time was adjusted by NMS - 0027 PowerReturn I Power returned after ARO - 0028 CcanSndFail W Sending car bus CAN message failed - 0029 CcanPubFail W Publishing car bus CAN message failed - 0030 CCanError W Disturbances on the car CAN bus have been detected.  Check termination, wiring, shielding - 0031 GcanTxFull W Group bus CAN messages cannot be sent DIS-CAN 0032 GcanBusOff F Group bus CAN communication failure DIS-CAN 0033 GcanSndFail W Sending group bus CAN message failed DIS-CAN 0034 GcanPubFail W Publishing group bus CAN message failed DIS-CAN 0035 GCanError W Disturbances on the group CAN bus have been detected.  Check termination, wiring, shielding DIS-CAN 0036 GwCommErr W Gateway communication failure REM-TYP=6,7,8 0037 Time Fault W The real time clock was corrupted and has now been reset to 01.01.2004.  Use M-1-3-8-1 to set the correct date/time - 0038 Time Nms>1h W Time synchronization between NMS and Controller greater than 1h - 0039 ZKIP Div0 W Numerical division by 0 during ZKIP au- thentication -

# Page 3^page=3
This document contains technical data subject to EAR. U.S. ECCN: EAR99 OTIS Engineering Center Berlin Software Basic Data GCS - GECB Service Tool Reference List No.: GP330782JAH_REF SCN: GP330782JAH Page: 53 / 102 Date: 2023-11-16 event class reason related config 0040 CarioComErr F No CAN communication to CARIO board (i.e. TOCB, COPG, CSPB)  Check CAN termination, wiring EN-CARIO>0 0041 Elite OOS W An Elite Service team has taken the car out of service via remote command  Follow instructions on the Service Panel - 0042 TimAdjByRTC I The GECB time was adjusted by the RTC 0043 Bad Params W Some parameters are out of range  Use M-3-2 to set the default values 0044 FdcbComErr W CAN-Communication to FDCB is not working.  check CAN-wiring  check CAN-LEDs on FDCB EN-FD=1 0045 FD-OCT W Damper A/B OverCurrent detected (0045 FD-OCT)  Adjust parameter <M-1-3-1-2> FdMaxOcT  Check damper wiring and solenoid current EN-FD=1 0046 FD-OHT W Damper OverHeated (0046 FD-OHT)  Check damper soleniod EN-FD=1 0047 FD-Check W 1) Damper A/B Position vs Command mismatch (0047 FD-Check)  Adjust parameter <M-1-3-1-2> FdStChgT  Check damper wiring and communication between GECB and FDCB (0047 FD-Check) 2) Damper A/B Temperature warning detected  Adjust parameter <M-1-3-1-2> FdTempWT EN-FD=1 0048 FramQTout F FRAM Queueing Timeout 0049 FramWTout F FRAM Waiting Timeout Operational Control Sub System 0100 OpMode NAV F OCSS is not available due to drive fault (e.g. drive-fault (NR). This mode is also active after INS before the COR starts -

# Page 4^page=4
This document contains technical data subject to EAR. U.S. ECCN: EAR99 OTIS Engineering Center Berlin Software Basic Data GCS - GECB Service Tool Reference List No.: GP330782JAH_REF SCN: GP330782JAH Page: 54 / 102 Date: 2023-11-16 event class reason related config 0101 EPO shutd. W The car is not able to run during EPO (e.g. because it is switched to ERO); the next car is allowed to recover. NU (017) NUSD (018) NUSG (019) 0102 OpMode DTC W door was not able to close (missing DCL, DFC, DW or GDS) within timeout Check UCM plug This can be caused if parameter EN- ABL is enabled at a unit without ABL DCL (694) RDCL (695) DOOR, REAR DTC-T EN-ABL 0103 OpMode DTO F door was not able to open (missing DOL) within timeout DOL (000) RDOL (544) DOOR, REAR DTO-T 0104 OpMode DCP W car was not able to answer a call within timeout (e.g. door was held open manual- ly) DCP-T 0106 PDS active I partition door switch is opened PDD (784) 0107 DS bypass F Malfunction of Disable Door Switch Re- lays (DDSR) for Generic Firemen Ser- vice. DDSRC (970) 0108 EEC bypass F Malfunction of Disable Emergency Exit Contact Relay (DEMERC) for Generic Firemen Service. DEMERC (971) 0109 Stuck DCB F Door Close Button contact permanent active DCB (003) 0110 Stuck RDCB F Rear Door Close Button contact perma- nent active RDCB (547) 0111 Stuck CB I Car Call Button contact permanent active 0112 Stuck HB I Hall Call Button contact permanent active 0113 NoCarLight F The light within the car has failed. 0114 OLD > 30s E The elevator is overloaded for more than 30 seconds. 0115 LNS > 30s E The elevator is full for more than 30 sec- onds. 0116 COR > 2min E The elevator is attempting to correct posi- tion for more than 2 minutes. 0117 INI > 2min E The elevator is initializing after a power on for more than 2 minutes. 0118 PRK > 2min E The elevator is performing a park run for more than 2 minutes. 0119 WCO > 5min E The elevator is performing wildcar opera- tion for more than 5 minutes.

# Page 5^page=5
This document contains technical data subject to EAR. U.S. ECCN: EAR99 OTIS Engineering Center Berlin Software Basic Data GCS - GECB Service Tool Reference List No.: GP330782JAH_REF SCN: GP330782JAH Page: 55 / 102 Date: 2023-11-16 event class reason related config 0120 NOR > 5min E The elevator is in NOR operation for more than 5 minutes. Could be the result of a stuck reversal device. 0121 OpMode ARO F Automatic Rescue Operation was activat- ed due to a power failure or via OFT test menu. 0122 SP:en.Feat! I One of the following features is pro- grammed in the E2PROM but not yet en- abled by Service Panel: EFO EPO CLR HBP  Use “Startup-Enable Features” to acti- vate these features. SPB-TYP=1: EFO-POS EPO-POS IO1000 CLR HBP-POS 0123 NAV > 60s E The elevator is in NAV mode for more than 60 seconds. This event is only logged when the system is in NAV mode and no OOS Blockage is detected for 60 seconds. This was increased in GAA30780GAD from 30 to 60 seconds. 0124 Stuck WDCB F Wheel Chair Door Close Button contact permanent active WDCB(1167) 0125 Stuck RWDCB F Rear Wheel Chair Door Close Button contact permanent active RWDCB(1171) 0126 EEQF Active I Detected Earthquake Flash from external system EQO=3, EEQFH (1213), EEQFG (1214) 0127 EQOR > Time W EQO restart condition active, but not ar- rived to the nearest floor within 4 minute 10 sec. EQO=3, EN-EQOR>0 0128 EQOR Hi-gal I EQO restart condition active with high- gal, but not run to the nearest floor within 10 sec. EQO=3 EN-EQOR>0 0129 EQAR Start I Started EQAR after EQO operation with just low-gal active EN-EQAR>0 0130 EQAR Abort W EQAR aborted by causes like EFO or EFS active, PFO active, other earthquake sensor active except for low-gal, discon- nected remote service under defined condition which EQAR allowed with re- mote service connection EN-EQAR>0, EQAR-O 0131 EQAR Fail F EQAR start was not permitted by drive for 60 sec after completion EQO general operation. EN-EQAR>0

# Page 6^page=6
This document contains technical data subject to EAR. U.S. ECCN: EAR99 OTIS Engineering Center Berlin Software Basic Data GCS - GECB Service Tool Reference List No.: GP330782JAH_REF SCN: GP330782JAH Page: 56 / 102 Date: 2023-11-16 event class reason related config 0132 EQAR OK I Allowed to return to normal operation af- ter completion of temporary serviceable check thru floor-to-floor, top to bottom and bottom-to-floor running. EN-EQAR>0 0133 EQAR > Time W No maintenance since EQAR for defined time EN-EQAR>0, EqrTrg-T 0134 OpMode EQO I Earthquake is occurred. EqoAlarm 0135 Event RFS I Selected events (max: 3) by SVT is de- tected for pre-determined time. RfsEvU-T RfsEvT-T RfsEvC-T RfsEvxGr RfsEvxId RfsEvOpx 0137 eCall Check I An unusual eCall situation has occurred. EN-ECALL 0138 MS-StuckOff F Passenger Detection motion sensor fault check failed, Motion sensor declared as faulty (permanently stuck-off) PD-SENS 0139 MS-StuckOn F Passenger Detection motion sensor fault check failed, Motion sensor declared as faulty (permanently stuck-on) PD-SENS 0140 LR Fault F LR feedback signal not plausible to the expected LR state GECBP Motion Control Sub System 0200 DrvPosInval W Drive has set the position invalid, result- ing in COR run. Refer to Drive Manual to check the poten- tial cause. - 0201 Correct.Run I A correction run was performed (e.g. after INS, NAV) Not GECBP 0202 /ES in FR W ES-input was activated during Fast Run - 0203 /ES in SR W ES-input was activated during Slow Run - 0204 TCI/ERO on I TCI or ERO has been switched on, or the RSL 30VDC is dead. ERO-TYP 0205 SE-Fault F The car cannot start due to missing SE- signal  Check the door bypass  Check GECB/LCB_IIC board. This error will be logged if a board for EN81-1/EN81-2 is used in an EN81-20 system. Not GECBP 0206 OTS active F OTS input has been activated Hydraulic: EN-OTS 0207 DDP in FR F no hoistway signals have been detected during Fast Run (missing DZ) LCB_IIC: DDP

# Page 7^page=7
This document contains technical data subject to EAR. U.S. ECCN: EAR99 OTIS Engineering Center Berlin Software Basic Data GCS - GECB Service Tool Reference List No.: GP330782JAH_REF SCN: GP330782JAH Page: 57 / 102 Date: 2023-11-16 event class reason related config 0207 DDP in FR F bolts or relays for ACD are not in ex- pected position Hydraulic: ACD-OPT 0208 DDP in SR F duration of Slow Run is longer than 3P- time (i.e. no stop signal has been detect- ed) LCB_IIC: 3P 0208 DDP in SR F bolts or relays for ACD are not in ex- pected position Hydraulic: ACD-OPT 0209 DDP in RS F duration of Rescue Run is longer than 3P-time (i.e. no stop signal has been de- tected) LCB_IIC: 3P 0209 DDP in RS F bolts or relays for ACD are not in ex- pected position Hydraulic: ACD-OPT 0210 /DZ in NST F DZ-signal was lost or not detected at all after having stopped DZ-TYP 0211 /DFC in FR W car door has been opened during Fast Run - 0212 /DFC in SR W car door has been opened during Slow Run - 0213 DrvPrepErr W PrepareToRun expected from Drive (e.g. missing loadweighing information for ABL) CAN Drive: DRV-TYP=1, <100 0214 DrvStuckPtr W Drive stuck in PrepareToRun CAN Drive: DRV-TYP=1, <100 0215 DrvStuckRtr W Drive stuck in ReadyToRun(NoAbl) CAN Drive: DRV-TYP=1, <100 0216 DrvBrakeErr F Brake not lifted. CAN Drive: DRV-TYP=1, <100 0217 DrvCreepErr F Drive Creeptime too long. CAN Drive: DRV-TYP=1, <100 0218 DrvShutdown F The drive reports a Shutdown  See event logging of Drive. CAN Drive: DRV-TYP=1, <100 0219 DrvStartErr F CAN Drive: DRV-TYP=0 0220 DrvService W The drive requested “Request For Ser- vice” (REM). See drive event logging for reason. CAN Drive: DRV-TYP=1, <100 0221 DrvStopErr F It was detected that the drive was stopped while it was supposed to run (so called “Running but Idle lockup”). To avoid a blockage, a correction run is initiated. CAN Drive: DRV-TYP=0 0222 1TH-Fault W The DBR-contact which is connected to the input 1TH is opened. The drive is powered down to allow cooling. GECB-EN, GECB-AP

# Page 8^page=8
This document contains technical data subject to EAR. U.S. ECCN: EAR99 OTIS Engineering Center Berlin Software Basic Data GCS - GECB Service Tool Reference List No.: GP330782JAH_REF SCN: GP330782JAH Page: 58 / 102 Date: 2023-11-16 event class reason related config 0223 2TH-Fault F One of the contacts which is connected to the 2TH is opened. GECB-EN, GECB_II, GECB_LV, GECB2_LV, GECBP 0224 DrvEndRun F Drive did not end the run. CAN Drive: DRV-TYP=1, <100 0225 110VAC dead F 110VAC is dead for 5 seconds. This event is logged when only the 110VAC is dead and also when the controller is powered off. - 0226 LS-fault F --> see description for blinking message - 0227 110Vac OffOn F Power supply (110VAC) died and came back before complete power down LCB_IIC, GECB-EN, GECB_II, GECB-AP, 0228 1LS+2LS on F Limit switches 1LS and 2LS are active at the same time.  Check power supply of the position reference system. Not GECBP 0229 ACS operat. F  see description for blinking message ‘ACD Fault!” LCB_IIC: Hydraulic: ACD/UXT 0230 TCI Adr Chk F Some RSL I/Os have to be programmed to a valid address and are not allowed to be set to 00-0 or 01-0. Currently only one I/O is checked: 691 – TCI  Set I/O 691 to a valid address Not GECBP: TCI(691) 0231 Drv SC Err F Indicates that ADO is enabled and the conditions were met to begin an ADO run, but the DriveSpeedCheck signal did not toggle on this run  Check CAN bus connection EN-ADO=1 0232 UCM Welded F The UCM relay is stuck.  Check hardware See also Feature Setup Description. EN-UCMK=1 0233 PowerSave I The drive package has been powered down PDR-D 0234 UCM Detect F Unintended Car Movement (leaving the door zone with open doors under motor) was detected by the GECB or by the Drive or both. For more information, see the Feature Setup Description. Not GECBP: UCM-TYP=1,2,3

# Page 9^page=9
This document contains technical data subject to EAR. U.S. ECCN: EAR99 OTIS Engineering Center Berlin Software Basic Data GCS - GECB Service Tool Reference List No.: GP330782JAH_REF SCN: GP330782JAH Page: 59 / 102 Date: 2023-11-16 event class reason related config 0235 ABL Abort W Advanced Brake Lift was aborted be- cause the door was not fully closed 10 seconds after the brake was lifted. EN-ABL=1, DRV-TYP=1 0236 Learn Run I The OVF20 has started its Learn Run. LCB_IIC: DRV-TYP = 102 0237 /DW in FR W landing door has been opened during Fast Run - 0238 /DW in SR W landing door has been opened during Slow Run - 0239 /ES with ][ E The ES signal activated (indicating that perhaps the TES or PES button was pushed) without seeing a prior “/DW in FR” or “/DW in SR” event. If a prior “/DW in FR” or “/DW in SR” event is seen, then that would be an indication of the normal procedure to enter the hoistway, so is not reported. Note that ES input is after a whole series of contacts. In addition to TES and PES, ES can become active as a result of the UDLS (Up/Down Limit Switch), the GTC (Governor Tension Contact), etc. Refer to the system wiring diagram to under- stand the complete set of contacts that can activate ES. - 0240 /DW > 5min E The DW signal is missing for 5 minutes, but no HAD and no DTC was detected. - 0241 /DFC > 5min E The DFC signal is missing for 5 minutes, but no HAD and no DTC was detected. - 0242 ETSC Fault F Emergency Terminal Slowdown Circuit has failed. Refer to Gxx30780DAC_Fsd for a description of this function Not GECBP: PX2.1=2 UP(576) DN(577) 0243 ETSC Block F System is blocked after ETSD Failure to prevent further runs. Not GECBP: PX2.1=2 UP(576) DN(577) 0244 2SE-Fault F The input 2nd Start Enable opened (e.g. active BID). GECB_II, GECB_LV, GECB2_LV, 0245 /UIB/DIB FR W The safety chain opened at UIB or DIB during a run while accelerating or at con- stant velocity. GECB-EN, GECB_II, GECB-AP, LCB_IIC

# Page 10^page=10
This document contains technical data subject to EAR. U.S. ECCN: EAR99 OTIS Engineering Center Berlin Software Basic Data GCS - GECB Service Tool Reference List No.: GP330782JAH_REF SCN: GP330782JAH Page: 60 / 102 Date: 2023-11-16 event class reason related config 0246 /UIB/DIB SR W The safety chain opened at UIB or DIB during a run while decelerating. GECB-EN, GECB_II, GECB-AP, LCB_IIC 0247 No UIB DIB F The safety chain stayed open at UIB and DIB for longer than 5 seconds.  In Low Overhead Installations (OHLOW), check KS contact GECB-EN, GECB_II, GECB-AP, LCB_IIC 0248 ES Wiring F ES Safety chain wiring fault (PES not wired in series)  check safety chain wiring. After correc- tion of the wiring the error is cleared by switching off/on.  For GCS222MRL, the SP function “Tools – Safety Wiring” must be execut- ed. Not GECBP 0249 SAF Wiring F SAF Safety chain wiring fault (DS/GS not wired in series)  check safety chain wiring. After correc- tion of the wiring the error is cleared by successful door operation or by unlocking the landing door.  For GCS222MRL, the SP function “Tools – Safety Wiring” must be execut- ed. Not GECBP 0250 6LS-TYP! W The parameter “6LS-TYP” at GECB has a different value than the parameter “6LS- TYP” at the drive.  Check GECB Parameter “6LS-TYP”  Check MCB3/MCB3x/GDCB Parame- ter “6LS-TYP” Not GECBP: 6LS-TYP 0251 DrvOverHeat F When the drive overheat signal is re- ceived from Drive thru CAN bus.  Check GDCB events EN-JIS>0 0252 UCMCommErr F CAN communication fault between GECB and UCM board  Check CAN line between GECB and UCM board UCM-TYP=2 0253 FSO Rly Err F When the inconsistency is detected for front SO relay  Check SO relay for front door  Check IO FDZ(1195), FSO(1197) and CHK_SO(1196) EN-SO>0 0254 RSO RlyErr F Not implemented

# Page 11^page=11
This document contains technical data subject to EAR. U.S. ECCN: EAR99 OTIS Engineering Center Berlin Software Basic Data GCS - GECB Service Tool Reference List No.: GP330782JAH_REF SCN: GP330782JAH Page: 61 / 102 Date: 2023-11-16 event class reason related config 0255 No RLV/ABL F An error occurred during releveling on a system with EN-required UCM. Further relevel runs will be prevented at the floor where the problem occurred. Relevel runs will be re-enabled when the car moves to another floor. UCM-TYP=3 0256 EtsdCommErr F The ET_S1-4 expectation message from the Drive was not received for 3 seconds.  check CAN-wiring  check CAN-LEDs Not GECBP: PX2.1=4 or 5 ET_P1(1257) ET_P2(1258) ET_S1(1259) ET_S2(1260) ET_P3(1261) ET_P4(1262) ET_S3(1263) ET_S4(1264) 0257x Etsd Block F ETSD with reed switch monitor function has detected a problem. This is the case if the ET_P1-4 signals do not match their expected value in a doorzone, or if the ET_S1-4 signals do not match their ex- pected values during the run, or if the configuration of the parameters and Ios is inconsistent.  check relays  check configuration and Ios  See subcode ‘x’ in Event Logging Subcodes section for details Not GECBP: PX2.1=4 or 5 ET_P1(1257) ET_P2(1258) ET_S1(1259) ET_S2(1260) ET_P3(1261) ET_P4(1262) ET_S3(1263) ET_S4(1264) 0258 /UIB/DIB ET W The safety chain opened between DFC and UIB/DIB within the first step ETSD zone, as indicated by ET_P1 and ET_P2.  verify Drive ETSD settings, “ETP Spe %DutySpe” and “ETP2 Spe %Duty”  verify ET_P1-4 sensor adjustment Not GECBP: PX2.1=4 or 5 ET_P1(1257) ET_P2(1258) ET_S1(1259) ET_S2(1260) ET_P3(1261) ET_P4(1262) ET_S3(1263) ET_S4(1264)

# Page 12^page=12
This document contains technical data subject to EAR. U.S. ECCN: EAR99 OTIS Engineering Center Berlin Software Basic Data GCS - GECB Service Tool Reference List No.: GP330782JAH_REF SCN: GP330782JAH Page: 62 / 102 Date: 2023-11-16 event class reason related config 0259 LateET_S2/4 W The ASCB-controlled relays, ET_S2 and/or ET_S4 switch open too late during acceleration in comparison to the GECB- controlled relays, ET_S1 and/or ET_S3.  readjust ASCB settings Not GECBP: PX2.1=4 or 5 ET_P1(1257) ET_P2(1258) ET_S1(1259) ET_S2(1260) ET_P3(1261) ET_P4(1262) ET_S3(1263) ET_S4(1264) 0260 8LS2 oper. F 8LS2 input was activated Note: Hydraulic controllers only LCB_IIC, Hydraulic 0261 PLS1 active F PLS1 input has been activated LCB_IIC, Hydraulic: EN-PLS 0262 Mot.Vlv tim F motorized valve got stuck LCB_IIC, Hydraulic: DRV-TYP=200 0263 DZ missed W UIS & DIS have been detected without DZ. This happens when one of LV relays is defect. LCB_IIC: EN-RLV 0264 J-Relay F The LCB_IIC detected a fault at the 3- line-power-supply (i.e. phases missing or turned) LCB_IIC: EN-J, J-T 0265 brake-fault F The brake current (hydro: valve current) was missing during run. LCB_IIC: BON-D, BFLT-T, BOFF-D 0267 LSVF-W: /DR F LSVF-W reported an error (Drive Not Ready) LCB_IIC: DRV_TYP=102 C-TYPE (see MCB) 0268 LSVF-W: /SC F During deceleration the speed of the car was too high to perform ADO or Relevel- ing. Therefore ADO and RLV are not al- lowed any more LCB_IIC: (see MCB) 0269 LSVF-W:P-ON F LSVF-W has been powered on LCB_IIC: P-OFF-D P-ON-D 0270 OP lost F OVF10 announced a drive problem by switching off the OP signal. Look into Event Logging on OVF10. LCB_IIC: DRV-TYP = 103 0271 BTI NG 3rd I BTI result is no good at every 3rd trial. REI-TYP 0272 BTI OK 3rd I BTI result is good at 3rd trial. REI-TYP 0273 BTI Fault I BTI is not available or aborted. REI-TYP

# Page 13^page=13
This document contains technical data subject to EAR. U.S. ECCN: EAR99 OTIS Engineering Center Berlin Software Basic Data GCS - GECB Service Tool Reference List No.: GP330782JAH_REF SCN: GP330782JAH Page: 63 / 102 Date: 2023-11-16 event class reason related config 0274 UCM Prot F F  For UCM-TYP=4: The UCM Safety Valve (RSL output SVC 1304) cannot block the downward movement. It could be a wiring failure near the RSL i/o’s 1304 – 1307.  For UCM-TYP=5: The iValve check is failed. It could be a wiring failure near the RSL i/o’s 1305 – 1306. LCB_IIC: DRV-TYP ≥ 200 UCM-TYP 0275 CR-FSO Flt W Physical address of additional front PRS input for COR rescue is invalid or ab- normally detected at the inter-floor or the unnecessary landing. Not GECBP: CR-OPT CR_FSO 0276 CR-RSO Flt W Physical address of additional rear PRS input for COR rescue is invalid or abnor- mally detected at the inter-floor or the unnecessary landing. Not GECBP: CR-OPT CR_RSO 0277 /MD in FR W The safety chain opened at MD/AES dur- ing a run while accelerating or at constant velocity. LCB_IIC MD/AES=1 0278 /MD in SR W The safety chain opened at MD/AES dur- ing a run while decelerating. LCB_IIC MD/AES=1 0279 /MD > 5min F The MD/AES signal is missing for 5 minutes, but no HAD and no DTC was detected. LCB_IIC MD/AES=1 0281 MPD F Motor Protection Device was active LCB_IIC 0282 /SCO in FR W The safety chain opened at SC_OUT dur- ing a run while accelerating or at constant velocity. GECB_LV, GECB2_LV, GECBP 0283 /SCO in SR W The safety chain opened at SC_OUT dur- ing a run while decelerating. GECB_LV, GECB2_LV, GECBP 0284 No SCO F The safety chain stayed open at SC_OUT for longer than 5 seconds.  In Low Overhead Installations (OHLOW), check KS contact  Check BYPASS plug GECB_LV, GECB2_LV, GECBP

# Page 14^page=14
This document contains technical data subject to EAR. U.S. ECCN: EAR99 OTIS Engineering Center Berlin Software Basic Data GCS - GECB Service Tool Reference List No.: GP330782JAH_REF SCN: GP330782JAH Page: 64 / 102 Date: 2023-11-16 event class reason related config 0285 /SCO ETSD W The safety chain opened between DFC and SC_OUT within the first step ETSD zone, as indicated by ET_P1 and ET_P2.  verify Drive ETSD settings, “ETP Spe %DutySpe” and “ETP2 Spe %Duty”  verify ET_P1-4 sensor adjustment GECB_LV, GECB2_LV: PX2.1=4 or 5 ET_P1(1257) ET_P2(1258) ET_S1(1259) ET_S2(1260) ET_P3(1261) ET_P4(1262) ET_S3(1263) ET_S4(1264) 0286 MroDirHeavy I During MRO, a rescue run into the heavy direction was started (Penthouse Rescue) MRO-DIR 0287 BSR Fault F The brake switch relay is stuck  see description for blinking message 'BSR/RR Flt' LVA-TYP 0288 COR Count F Too many consecutive correction runs occured. This fault is remembered even if the power is lost.  To clear the fault, switch to ERO and back to NOR. Then the car will automati- cally move to the floor where the fault was detected and opens the door.  When the above method is not possi- ble (e.g. if the Allowed Mask was mispro- grammed before), perform a DCS run. - 0303 DBP-Fault F  see description for blinking message ‘DBP-Fault’ LCB_IIC: EN-RLV, DRV-TYP 0304 DOL:alw. U.S. ECCN: EAR99 OTIS Engineering Center Berlin Software Basic Data GCS - GECB Service Tool Reference List No.: GP330782JAH_REF SCN: GP330782JAH Page: 77 / 102 Date: 2023-11-16 event class reason related config 0754 Bcb3HwFail F The 3rd BCB published an EEPROM er- ror.  Replace LVPB (BOOSTC) LVA-TYP>=2 0755 PS3 >Volt F The 3rd LVPB detected overvoltage in the 230 VAC to 48 VDC converter LVA-TYP>=2 0756 PS3 >VFail F The 3rd LVPB’s 230 VAC to 48 VDC con- verter has been switched off because it persistently produced overvoltage  Replace LVPB (BOOSTC) LVA-TYP>=2 0757 ARD Error F Error was detected Commercial Automat- ic Rescue Device for Korea  Check LEDs on ARD device for error code Commercial ARD 0763x EncToutRst F The Rescue Encoder Timeout for MRO was reset to default value. See Event Logging Subcodes for Details.  Repeat Construction test: “Test MRO (Brake)” SPB-TYP=1 MCSS subsystem (continued 0200-0299) 0800x Belt-Fault F RBILW device is missing or has detected faulty belts. Refer to subcode  Check CAN connection  Check Belts LW-TYP=7 (RBILW) 0809x Brk-Supply F The Rescue Encoder detected a speed > 0.8 m/s while the car is supposed to run at slow speed. This indicates a serious wiring mistake at the supply for the brake!  Check wiring of the brake LVA-TYP=0 0812x CSB SCS F The RBI detected AREOB (Abnormal Relative Extension of One Belt) and opened the SCS contact. LW-TYP=7 (RBILW) 0814 /ADW in FR F ADW contact opened during Fast Run EN81-20 ACG 0815 /ADW in SR F ADW contact opened during Slow Run EN81-20 ACG 0816 /ADW > 5min F ADW contact opened for more than 5 minutes (potentially a door blockage) EN81-20 ACG 0821 DrvSafFail F Drive waits for safety chain, even the GECB detects a fully closed safety chain for >15s. DRV-TYP=1 SAFETY subsystem 0900 No SftyMsg W Periodical Safety Message from the safe- ty functional system missing  Check safety functionality GECBP

This document contains technical data subject to EAR. U.S. ECCN: EAR99 OTIS Engineering Center Berlin Software Basic Data GCS - GECB Service Tool Reference List No.: GP330782JAH_REF SCN: GP330782JAH Page: 78 / 102 Date: 2023-11-16 event class reason related config 0902 24hTstFail F The 24-Selftest of the SAFE system has failed  Check SAFE Event Log for further de- tails2 GECBP 0903 SAFE Event L Event from the SAFE system received  Check SAFE Event Log for further de- tails2 GECBP 0904 SAFE Block F Blockage from the SAFE system received  Check SAFE Event Log for further de- tails2 GECBP 0905 FloorTblFlt F The system floor table, which is shared between GECB, Drive and SAFE system, is not in sync between these components. One or more of the following steps are needed: - Load Certificate - Set Top Pos Landing - Drive Learn Run - DCS GECBP 0906 SAFE Fault F Integrity Blockage from the SAFE system received  Check SAFE Event Log for further de- tails2 GECBP 0907x BadExpDate F Expiration Date of the safety system can- not be calculated  See subcode ‘x’ in Event Logging Subcodes section for details GECBP 0915 Pit Access F PESSRAL has detected access to the pit If the SAB is powered off (e.g. by switch- ing off OCB and RPS) while the car is located above bottom landing, the SAB cannot monitor the DS0/RDS0 signals and will enter "Pit Access" for safety rea- sons. Activate and deactivate PADR keyswitch GECBP 0916 ESA Tripped F The SAFE system has reported that the Electronic Safeties Actuator has tripped. The magnets are dropped on the rail.  Clear using the “ESA Reset” function of the SERVICE menu on the SP GECBP 2 SAFE Events are described in the REF document of the safety system.
